meta.title: Featured Post
meta.description: A content-with-media style section that features a selected blog post, showing its topic tag, title, excerpt, and featured image.

tsx: import { shouldDisplay, cn, fetchMedia, renameBlock } from '@taktdev/utilities';

requiredPatterns.usage: Show placeholder content in editor when block is selected but post not yet chosen
Conditional class names for isReversed, darkMode, reverseRows
Post selector in InspectorControls sidebar with limit=1 for single post selection
Fetch selected post data (title, excerpt, featured image, categories) in editor
Rename block in list view to match selected post title
Fetch post featured image media in editor

qualityChecklist: Column reversal via isReversed and md:col-start-* pattern (matching ContentWithMedia)
Toolbar button for column reversal (flipHorizontal icon)
Toolbar button for row reversal (flipVertical icon)
Toolbar button for dark mode (shadow icon)
Topic tag pill with accent color styling
Post title rendered as H2 with text-header-2
Post excerpt rendered as body text
Button links to the post permalink
Featured image with aspect-[4/3] and rounded-3xl
Dark mode with charcoal card pseudo-elements
shouldDisplay() for empty state in editor
TSX cn() and PHP class_name() have identical conditional logic

attributes.name: selectedPost
attributes.description: The ID of the selected blog post to feature
attributes.validation: Must be a valid published post ID

attributes.name: isReversed
attributes.description: Reverses column order. false = media left (content right), true = media right (content left)

attributes.name: reverseRows
attributes.description: Reverses row order on mobile. false = media above content, true = content above media

attributes.name: darkMode
attributes.description: Enables dark mode with charcoal card backgrounds

attributes.name: buttonLabel
attributes.description: The label text for the CTA button that links to the post

attributes.name: headingSize
attributes.description: Controls the heading level. 'regular' renders H2 (text-header-2), 'small' renders H3 (text-header-3)

dynamicData.description: This block is a dynamic/server-side rendered block for the frontend (PHP). The editor (TSX) uses useSelect to fetch post data live. The PHP template fetches the post data server-side.

postDataUsed.title: Rendered as the block heading (H2 or H3 depending on headingSize)
postDataUsed.excerpt: Rendered as the description text below the title
postDataUsed.featuredImage: Rendered in the media column with aspect-[4/3] and rounded corners
postDataUsed.categories: First category rendered as topic tag pill above the eyebrow area

container.classes: container grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-x-16 gap-y-10 sm:gap-y-16 items-stretch

contentInner.description: Inner wrapper for content elements (topic tag row, heading, description, button). Replaces ThemeHeading since we need custom layout with topic tag pill.

topicTagRow.description: Row containing the topic tag pill and eyebrow text

topicTagPill.classes: inline-flex items-center justify-center px-2 py-1 rounded-full bg-accent-lighter border border-accent text-sm font-medium uppercase leading-[1.1] whitespace-nowrap shrink-0
topicTagPill.description: Pill/badge showing the post's first category (topic). Uses accent color tokens so it adapts to section color scheme.

eyebrowText.description: Optional eyebrow text next to the topic tag. This is NOT editable -- it comes from the post's category/topic name. Per the Figma, the eyebrow text area says 'Work Integrated Learning Initiative' which suggests it could be a second category or a custom field. However, analyzing the spec more carefully, this appears to be an editable eyebrow like the existing ContentWithMedia. Decision: keep it as an editable eyebrow field for flexibility.

heading.description: Post title rendered as heading. Not editable -- comes from selected post.

description.description: Post excerpt. Not editable -- comes from selected post.

conditionalClasses.btn-primary: darkMode === true (dark mode, white button -- handled by .dark .btn-primary CSS)

button.description: CTA button linking to the post. Label is editable (default: 'Learn More'). URL comes from post permalink.

imageWrapper.description: Container for the post featured image. Uses 4:3 aspect ratio matching ContentWithMedia single image layout.

image.description: Post featured image. In TSX uses fetchMedia + ImageDropUploader (read-only display, no upload). In PHP uses wp_get_attachment_image.

playOverlay.description: The Figma shows a play overlay on the image. Per spec, video is not needed. However, since the Figma consistently shows the play button overlay on all 4 variations, this may be decorative in the Figma mockup or the post may contain video content. Per spec: 'There is no need for video support.' -- we will NOT include a play button overlay.

mobile.behavior: Single column stack. Media appears above content by default (reverseRows=false). Topic tag pill and eyebrow remain in a horizontal row. Heading and text are full width. Dark mode uses -inset-(--side-gutter) for card pseudo-element padding.

tablet.behavior: Transitions to two-column grid at md (768px). Column order controlled by isReversed and md:col-start-* classes.

desktop.behavior: Two-column grid with gap-x-16 (64px). Content vertically centered (items-center on content column wrapper). Dark mode uses -inset-8 for card pseudo-element padding.

emptyState.noPostSelected: Show InspectorControls panel prompting to select a post. In the content area, show placeholder text: 'Select a post to feature'. The block should still render the structure with shouldDisplay() showing placeholders.
emptyState.postDeleted: PHP template should check if post exists and is published. If not, skip rendering entirely.

editorBehavior.blockNaming: Use renameBlock() to sync block name with selected post title
editorBehavior.postPreview: In the editor, fetch and display the selected post's title, excerpt, featured image, and first category in real-time using useSelect + getEntityRecord
editorBehavior.buttonLabelEditing: The button label is editable via RichText inline. Default is 'Learn More'. The button URL is not editable -- it always links to the selected post's permalink.

topicTagPill.description: Informational badge, no interactive role needed

image.alt: Post featured image alt text (from WordPress media library)

decisions.decision: Clone ContentWithMedia layout structure (two-column grid, isReversed via md:col-start-*, reverseRows, darkMode pattern)
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'apply all the rules that were used on the existing content with media block, you can basically clone it and then do the changes on top of it'

decisions.decision: Replace editable heading with post title (read-only in editor, dynamic in PHP)
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'it will show the post title instead of having an editable title'

decisions.decision: Replace editable description with post excerpt (read-only in editor, dynamic in PHP)
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'it will show the post excerpt instead of the description'

decisions.decision: Replace eyebrow with topic tag pill showing first category name
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'it will have a pill with the topic tag instead of an eyebrow'. Figma shows a rounded pill with green background and border containing 'Topic Tag Goes Here' (node 811:59179)

decisions.decision: Button links to post permalink with editable label (default: 'Learn More')
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'the button will link to the post' and 'the button label should be editable, with Learn More as the default text'

decisions.decision: Media column shows only post featured image, no video support, no gallery layout
decisions.evidence: Spec says: 'the media is the post featured image. There is no need for video support.'

decisions.decision: Use PostSelectorSortable component with limit=1 for single post selection in InspectorControls
decisions.evidence: PostSelectorSortable from @taktdev/components supports limit=1 which returns a single number (not array). Used in CBM DynamicContent block.

decisions.decision: Topic tag pill uses accent color tokens (bg-accent-lighter, border-accent) for automatic color scheme adaptation
decisions.evidence: Figma node 811:59179 shows bg-[#eeffb5] (neon-green-20/lighter) with border-[#d4ff45] (neon-green). These map to accent-lighter and accent tokens, allowing automatic adaptation when section color is changed.

decisions.decision: Image uses aspect-[4/3] matching ContentWithMedia single image layout
decisions.evidence: Figma shows image at 624x677 (~0.92 ratio close to 4:3). ContentWithMedia single image uses aspect-[4/3]. Maintaining consistency.

decisions.decision: Do NOT use ThemeHeading component. Build custom content layout with topic tag pill, post title, excerpt, and button inline.
decisions.evidence: ThemeHeading expects editable RichText fields for eyebrow/heading/description/buttons. This block needs read-only post data display with a custom topic tag pill. Custom layout is more appropriate than trying to force ThemeHeading to work differently.

decisions.decision: Keep an optional editable eyebrow field alongside the topic tag pill
decisions.evidence: Figma node 811:59178 shows both a topic tag pill AND an eyebrow text ('Work Integrated Learning Initiative') side by side. The eyebrow provides additional context beyond the topic tag. Making it editable allows flexibility.

decisions.decision: No play button overlay on the featured image
decisions.evidence: Spec explicitly states: 'There is no need for video support.' The play overlay in Figma appears to be a leftover from the ContentWithMedia component it was based on.

decisions.decision: Dark mode topic tag pill keeps its accent colors (no inversion needed) since the pill has its own background
decisions.evidence: Figma dark variant (node 811:59232) shows the topic tag pill with the same neon-green background colors, even in dark mode. The pill text stays charcoal on the green background.

decisions.decision: In dark mode, eyebrow text color changes to white
decisions.evidence: Figma dark variant (node 811:59236) shows eyebrow text with text-primary (white) color, while light variant (node 811:59183) shows charcoal color. The dark class handles this automatically.

variations.name: Default - No Post Selected
variations.purpose: Tests empty state when no post is selected

variations.name: Light Mode - Media Left (Default)
variations.purpose: Full content with media on left, content on right

variations.name: Light Mode - Media Right (Reversed)
variations.purpose: Tests isReversed with media on right

variations.name: Dark Mode - Media Left
variations.purpose: Tests dark mode with charcoal card backgrounds

attributes.buttonLabel: View Full Article on Our Blog

variations.name: Small Heading Size
variations.purpose: Tests headingSize='small' which renders H3 instead of H2

variations.name: With Eyebrow
variations.purpose: Tests the optional eyebrow text next to the topic tag

tsxEditor.postDataFetching: Use useSelect with getEntityRecord('postType', 'post', attributes.selectedPost, { _embed: true }) to fetch the selected post with embedded featured media and terms. Extract: post.title.rendered, post.excerpt.rendered (strip HTML tags for display), post._embedded['wp:featuredmedia'][0].id for fetchMedia, post._embedded['wp:term'][0][0] for first category (topic tag).
tsxEditor.postSelector: Use PostSelectorSortable from @taktdev/components in InspectorControls PanelBody. Set limit={1} and postTypes={['post']}. When limit=1, the component returns a single number (post ID), not an array.
tsxEditor.contentDisplay: Post title, excerpt, and topic tag are displayed as read-only text (not RichText). Only the buttonLabel uses RichText for inline editing. Use AdvancedRichText from @taktdev/components for the button label.
tsxEditor.blockNaming: Call renameBlock() whenever the selected post changes, using the post title as the block name.
tsxEditor.featuredImage: Use fetchMedia(post featured image ID) to get the image data, then display with ImageDropUploader in read-only mode (no onSelect handler) OR simply render an tag since the image is not user-uploadable.
tsxEditor.emptyState: When no post is selected, show a placeholder message in the content area prompting the user to select a post from the sidebar.

phpFrontend.postDataFetching: Use get_post($selectedPost) to verify the post exists and is published. Use get_the_title($selectedPost), get_the_excerpt($selectedPost), get_post_thumbnail_id($selectedPost), get_the_category($selectedPost), get_permalink($selectedPost).
phpFrontend.topicTag: Get first category with get_the_category($selectedPost). If category has accent_color meta, apply it as a class on the section or pill.
phpFrontend.earlyReturn: If $selectedPost is empty or the post doesn't exist/isn't published, return early without rendering.
phpFrontend.buttonUrl: Always use get_permalink($selectedPost) as the button URL.

blockJson.renderCallback: This block needs PHP server-side rendering for the dynamic post data. Use 'render' key in block.json pointing to 'FeaturedPost.php'.
blockJson.example: For block preview, use a placeholder image URL in a custom preview attribute structure.
